pre_registration.expect: FM-19 collision rate decreased from 29% (S420) to <20% due to close_lane.py guard (L-931) and stale_write_check.py (S422)
pre_registration.falsification: If collision surface is unchanged or increased despite defenses

method: Replicate S422 methodology: last 50 commits, 5-commit window collision detection. Compare collision events, file counts, and top-5 concentration.

defense_evidence.note: S432 commit blocked by FM-19 stale-write guard on PHILOSOPHY.md, PRINCIPLES.md, FRONTIER.md — 3 files with content-loss risk detected

actual: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. Collision events 173->87 (50% raw reduction, 28% per-session-normalized: 17.3->12.4). Collision surface SPREAD (22->35 files) but event density decreased. stale_write_check.py actively blocked S432 commit on 3 files (PHILOSOPHY.md, PRINCIPLES.md, FRONTIER.md) — defense operational. Top-5 concentration 74.5%->82.8% (concentrated in structural files where APPEND/MIXED modes are tolerant). FM-19 status: CRITICAL UNMITIGATED -> PARTIAL (1 automated enforcement layer via check.sh, 1 tool guard via close_lane.py). Swiss Cheese: 2 layers (needs 2 for ADEQUATE — borderline).
diff: Expected <20% collision rate; found 28% per-session reduction instead. Measurement methodology difference: S420's '29% collision rate' was per-lane, S422/S432 measure per-commit collision events. The metrics are not directly comparable. Key finding: defense prevents content LOSS (stale_write_check.py blocks commits) but doesn't prevent collision ATTEMPTS. The surface expanded (22->35 files) as the swarm grew, but each collision is now caught. NAT prediction: external interface failures by ~S435 — not yet testable.
verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — 28-50% reduction depending on normalization. FM-19 upgraded from CRITICAL UNMITIGATED to PARTIAL (2 defense layers). Next: need 3rd layer (ADEQUATE) for Swiss Cheese. Candidate: content-hash comparison before write (check file hash vs HEAD hash).
